rationale: SPY is 7.8% above its 200-day average and trades at a stretched valuation (trailing P/E ~25, Shiller CAPE ~40). The 10-year Treasury yield sits at 4.57%, near a 4-week high. The U.S.-Iran conflict escalated overnight with fresh strikes, pushing Brent crude to $78/barrel and introducing new inflation-via-energy risk. FOMC minutes released July 8 show officials divided on rate direction — mildly less hawkish than the dot plot implied, but not a clear all-clear. June CPI on July 14 is the next major catalyst and the Cleveland Fed nowcast puts it at ~3.96% YoY. No candidate in our watchlist offers a meaningful positive margin of safety.
events: June 2026 CPI release Jul 14 8:30am ET — Cleveland Fed nowcast ~3.96% YoY
key_risks: June CPI (Jul 14) expected ~3.96% YoY — a hot print would reprice rate hike odds sharply
Iran war escalation pushing Brent crude to $78/barrel, adding new inflation pressure via energy
10-year Treasury yield at 4.57% near 4-week high — elevated cost of capital caps equity valuations
Market valuation stretched: Shiller CAPE ~40 is 125% above historical average; trailing P/E ~25 vs 18 median
NY Fed consumer inflation expectations near 3-year highs — Fed cannot ease if expectations are unanchored
Next FOMC meeting July 29 is a live risk event with committee divided on rate direction
fomc_minutes_july8_summary: Officials divided; most participants see fed funds rate unchanged or slightly below current level by year-end — a marginal dovish tilt vs. June dot plot, but no commitment to cuts
note: Posture maintained CAUTIOUS from prior day (Jul 7). The one development that marginally softened the picture is the FOMC minutes tone — 'unchanged or slightly below current level by year-end' is less alarming than 9 of 19 dots pointing to hikes. But Iran war escalation, elevated oil, a 4.57% 10-year yield, and the June CPI release five days away keep the risk skew firmly to the downside for stocks. Our three researched candidates (AAPL at -60% MoS, MSFT at +0.2% MoS, V at -17% MoS) offer no actionable entry in a cautious posture. Hold cash.
